key_insight: A microstructure-aware convolutional deep learning model can bridge atomistic molecular dynamics and continuum finite-element simulations to predict shock-to-detonation behavior in energetic materials.
engineering_relevance: This enables parameter-free, multiscale prediction of run-to-detonation in nanostructured composites, which could improve safety, design, and performance assessment of energetic materials.
method: Train MISTnetX on large-scale MD simulations of shock propagation through complex microstructures.
Use the network to learn shock-microstructure interactions, hotspot formation, and deflagration onset.
Inject the learned sub-grid physics into coupled FE models for mechanics, thermal transport, shock, and chemistry.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to a synthetic but realistic nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composite, where the trained model enables full run-to-detonation prediction.
watch_item: The approach is demonstrated on a synthetic yet realistic composite, so generalization to different energetic materials and microstructures will need further validation.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
